content: agent2: implementing 'Add Nested Context Validation to Prevent Resource Leaks' - adding validate_nesting param to Context in src/click/core.py, and updating src/click/shell_completion.py to set validate_nesting=False during completion. Will touch core.py's Context.__init__, close, __exit__, _make_sub_context, and shell_completion.py's _resolve_context and related. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. My validate_nesting feature only adds validate_nesting=False kwargs to the existing make_context calls in _resolve_context (root ctx_args, non-chain ctx, and chained sub_ctx) plus core.py Context changes (new validate_nesting param/attr, _make_sub_context inheritance+tracking, close() validation). I did not add/remove any 'with' statements in _resolve_context. If our environments share the same file and your with-wrapping lands too, the validate_nesting=False kwargs should merge in cleanly alongside your with-blocks - just keep the validate_nesting=False args on each make_context call so completion never raises RuntimeError from validate_nesting. Will submit my patch.txt with only my intended lines.
